steps.text: Direct questions regarding appropriate collaborative activities to the Office of the General Counsel (OGC), CDC Branch, Department of Health & Human Services (HHS).
Refer questions and issues relating to gifts to individual employees from organizations outside the federal government to the Human Resources Office (HRO), CDC Ethics and Compliance Activity.

constraints.text: Gifts from foreign governments or international organizations, if authorized by the agency, are subject to the Foreign Gifts and Decorations Act (5 U.S.C. Section 7342).
Gifts to individual employees from organizations outside the federal government are subject to the government-wide ethics regulations.

steps.text: Accept monetary gifts in lieu of gifts in kind when feasible.

Deposit the accepted money into the general fund of the Treasury as soon as practicable, unless the funds constitute an authorized repayment or the agency has specific statutory authority to retain the funds.

preconditions.text: The conditions imposed by the donor must not be illegal, contrary to public policy, unreasonable to administer, contrary to generally accepted public standards, or present an actual or apparent conflict of interest.

constraints.text: An agency may not augment its appropriation from an outside source without specific statutory authority.

steps.text: Determine whether acceptance of a gift would compromise or appear to compromise the integrity of CDC or any of its employees, require CDC to undertake activities unrelated to its mission, or exert influence over its priorities.
Consider the identity of the immediate donor and, if applicable, the identity of any entity that funds the donor.
Review the gift for conflicts of interest using the established process.

preconditions.text: CDC is utilizing its gift acceptance authority to determine whether to accept a gift.

constraints.text: A government agency may not accept for its own use gifts of money or other property in the absence of specific statutory authority.

steps.text: Prepare letters of acceptance for a conditional gift.
Accept and utilize, on behalf of the federal government, any gift, donation, bequest, or devise of real or personal property from the CDC Foundation to aid or facilitate CDC work.
Accept and utilize funds from the CDC Foundation without regard to whether the funds are designated as general-purpose funds (unconditional) or special purpose funds (conditional).

preconditions.text: If, in the opinion of OGC, there is a valid question about the classification of the gift, then it will be deemed to be conditional.

constraints.text: In all circumstances, gift funds are collected in advance of use.

steps.text: Transfer income from lease or hire of accepted property to OFR for deposit with the Secretary of the Treasury.
Transfer proceeds from the liquidation of personal property to OFR for deposit with the Secretary of the Treasury.
Request that the executor liquidate the assets and make distribution in cash.
Forward copies of wills and trusts naming CDC as beneficiary and letters of transmittal to OGC.
Acknowledge receipt of the will or trust promptly.
Notify the Deputy Chief Financial Officer (CFO) when the estate is settled.
Enter proceeds from the wills or trusts in the accounts.

preconditions.text: Income from lease or hire of property accepted as gifts is received.
Proceeds from the liquidation of personal property are received.
A bequest or legacy is all or part of a general estate of a deceased individual.
Proceeds from wills or trusts are realized.

constraints.text: Transfer must occur within 24 hours of receipt.

steps.text: Conduct conflict of interest review at CDC for the gift.
Conduct a separate, independent conflict of interest review by the CDC Foundation.
Vet donors for projects managed at the CDC Foundation using the same standard.

preconditions.text: The gift is from the CDC Foundation.
The project is managed at the CDC Foundation, even when no funds come to CDC.

constraints.text: Funds received by or for the United States Government or its agencies must be deposited in, and administered through, United States Government accounts and may not be deposited with or otherwise administered by other persons or organizations.

An outside organization (including a foundation whose mission is to support CDC) may not serve as a financial intermediary for a third party in the donation of funds, equipment, supplies, or other resources.

steps.text: Document the review, the donor, and a description of the in-kind gift.
Estimate and record the monetary value of the gift in the review documentation, if possible.
Consult with OGC.
Request a CDC COI Panel Review if further elevation is required.

preconditions.text: The CIO makes a determination that there is a potential conflict of interest concern.

steps.text: Conduct an abbreviated conflict-of-interest review process including a review from OGC and the Ethics Desk of the EOC.
Complete a Gift Acceptance Validity Survey (Appendix 2) by the Office of the Director.
Relay any further considerations back to OGC, the Ethics Desk, and the CDC Foundation as appropriate.
Follow a conflict-of-interest review process for potential gifts and donors (CDC Foundation).
Perform an extra layer of scrutiny including completion of the Gift Acceptance Validity Survey (Appendix 2) before accepting a gift.

preconditions.text: When the Emergency Operations Center (EOC) is activated for a public health emergency.
When there is a potential Prohibited Source, or there is need for additional review.
If a gift is offered by a prohibited source (whether a CDC Foundation gift or a direct gift).

constraints.text: A prohibited source is any individual or entity that: Is seeking official action by CDC; Does business or seeks to do business with CDC; Conducts activities regulated by CDC; Has interests that may be substantially affected by performance or nonperformance of an employee's official duties; Benefits from work performed by CDC, such that they can use it to promote their business; Is an organization, a majority of whose members are described in Section VI. I. 2 (5 C.F.R. Section 2635.203(d)).

steps.text: Return tangible items to the donor

Pay the donor market value (retail cost of an item or service of like quality), such as paying the printed face value of the ticket, or the amount suggested as a contribution or donation to gain entrance to an event
Destroy, share within the office, or donate items
Advise donors from commercial organizations that the acceptance of a gift does not constitute an implied or direct endorsement

preconditions.text: Subject to OGC approval as a legally permissible use of appropriated funds
Subject to approval of a supervisor or organization head
If the donor is from a commercial organization

constraints.text: CDC will not accept a gift that is conditioned upon any expenditure that cannot be covered by the gift itself or from income earned from the gift, unless an Act of Congress has approved such expenditure.
Offers of property will not be accepted if the total costs associated with acceptance are expected to exceed the cost of purchasing a similar item and the cost of normal care and maintenance.
Gifts may not be accepted if the conditions placed on the gift create a conflict of interest.
Gifts tendered in order to secure an implied or direct endorsement are not accepted.

steps.text: Examine the precise terms of the statute authorizing the agency to accept the gift.
Follow limitations imposed by the authorizing statute.
Use a portion of the gift funds to pay employee salary and benefits.

preconditions.text: The donor does not express, directly or indirectly, an expectation of receiving a future benefit.
The gift is not a conditional gift of real property (unless accepted by the Secretary, HHS/ASFR).
It is determined that the proposed use will not conflict with the terms of the agency's authorizing statute.
The employee performs work associated with administering the gift or carrying out the work of the gift.
The gift conditions do not create a conflict of interest.

constraints.text: Gift funds can only be used to further authorized agency purposes and in accordance with the terms of the donation.
The employee’s total salary must remain consistent with applicable statutes and regulations governing federal employee compensation.
Only the salary of term employees should be paid using gift funds due to the finite nature of gift funds.

steps.text: Obtain pre-approval from OFR for gift funds expenditures for entertainment.
Consult with the OFR or the Ethics and Compliance Activity Office for assistance with sponsored travel.

preconditions.text: The entertainment furthers a valid, authorized function of CDC.
The function could not be otherwise accomplished from the government’s viewpoint without the expenditure.
The expenditure does not violate any restrictions imposed by the donor on the use of the funds.
The expenditure meets the necessary expense test.

constraints.text: Donated funds may not be used for entertainment that does not bear a legitimate official purpose, such as an office holiday party.
Once received by CDC, gift funds become public funds and should be spent in the same manner as appropriated funds.
The same regulations and procedures in effect for travel supported by appropriated funds apply for gift funds.

steps.text: Determine whether the funds are intended for CDC or for the employee serving in an individual capacity.
Accept the funds as a gift and deposit them into the appropriate CDC gift fund account.
Seek guidance from the CDC Ethics and Compliance Activity to avoid the potential for conflict of interest or other impropriety.
Seek guidance from OGC to ensure that conditions set out in grant award documents and agreements are legally appropriate.

preconditions.text: Unsolicited grants or other types of awards are made to CDC employees for scientific or other professional purposes.
An employee receives funds from any outside organization that supports the costs of CDC activities.

constraints.text: All statutory requirements regarding the acceptance of conditional gifts must be met.
Grant funds must be used to advance CDC’s mission, and there must be no legal objections to CDC’s acceptance of these grants.
Such a gift should not be a gift to the employee.

CDC policy prohibits employees, either directly or through a third party, from requesting or suggesting donations to CDC or any of its components, except grant funds as authorized by the Comptroller General.

steps.text: Assign monetary or gifts in kind received by the Office of the Director to the referenced CIO for coordinated handling.
Handle the responsibilities via the Office of the Chief of Staff (OCoS) as outlined for CIOs in section 4.B.

preconditions.text: When a CIO is not specified for a gift

constraints.text: CDC does not accept cash in the physical form of currency such as banknotes and coins.
CDC is not authorized to expend conditional gift funds to support functions not stated within the terms of the conditions of the gift.

steps.text: Forward cash or cash equivalents for deposit to the Deputy Chief Financial Officer within 24 hours of receipt, accompanied by a brief memorandum stating the gift is pre-decisional and any accompanying documentation.
Mail gift fund checks to the designated USPS or Private Carrier address.
Transmit wired funds via pay.gov.
OFR deposits the gift in a suspense account.
OFR returns monetary gift checks more than 90 days old to the recipient organization for return to the donor for reissuance.

preconditions.text: The gift is not officially accepted within the 24-hour period.
Monetary gift checks are more than 90 days old.

constraints.text: Must be in accordance with the Cash Management Policy, CDC-FM-2009-02.

steps.text: Thank the donor and inform them that the donation will be considered for acceptance and they will be advised of a decision at a later timeframe.
Notify the Logistics Management Branch, OSSAM, of proposals for personal property gifts, including a description, the accountable area, and the purpose specified by the donor.
Submit a copy of the document accepting the gift to OSSAM.
Create a basic record in the on-line property system, including estimated value, manufacturer's serial number, month/year received, physical location, CDC decal number, and purpose.
Determine at least annually if there will be excess funds not used within a 12-month period.
Request in writing that the Chief Financial Officer invest the excess funds in interest-bearing obligations of the United States, including information on when investments will be redeemed.

preconditions.text: Upon receipt of the personal property gift
Upon notification of acceptance of the gift by the CIO property custodian
If it is determined that there are excess funds and the amount of these funds is $50,000 or more

steps.text: Return the monies to the same gift fund account upon liquidation of investments.
Align the spend plan with the investment of the securities and redemption of the securities.
Conduct the Gift Acceptance Validity Survey to determine whether CDC's interest in accepting a gift outweighs any concern.
Give considerable scrutiny to all survey responses answered unfavorably.

preconditions.text: Gift funds must be received prior to obligation or expenditure.

constraints.text: If an investment is redeemed before the maturity date, there may be loss of principal.
Invested gift funds and income from these investments retain the same limitations as the original gifts.

steps.text: Obtain signature of Recommending Official.
Obtain signature of Approving Official.
Prepare Letter of Acceptance for Conditional Gifts.
Obtain donor signature and date for the use of excess funds acknowledgement.
Prepare Letter of Acceptance for Unconditional Gifts.

preconditions.text: The CIO Director is the signature authority

constraints.text: Use CIO letterhead if the CIO Director is the signature authority.
